title: Documentation And SOPs
summary: Use AI to document work more clearly, improve process clarity, and turn tribal knowledge into usable team assets.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Why Documentation Is A Strong Fit
lessons.subtitle: Documentation work usually starts with scattered knowledge and rough inputs.
lessons.objective: Identify documentation tasks where AI can reduce friction without reducing accuracy.
lessons.useWhen: Documentation work usually starts with scattered knowledge and rough inputs.
lessons.whyItMatters: Documentation often stalls because the work feels time-consuming and messy. AI helps most when the job is organizing and clarifying existing knowledge.

coreConcepts.title: Draft From Existing Knowledge
coreConcepts.text: Recordings, notes, process bullets, and examples make documentation much easier to generate.

coreConcepts.title: Clarity Beats Elegance
coreConcepts.text: The goal of SOPs and FAQs is usability, not impressive prose.

coreConcepts.title: Accuracy Still Comes First
coreConcepts.text: AI can create a first structure, but the people closest to the process must validate the steps.

workedExample.scenario: A team has an undocumented refund process known only by two operators.
workedExample.approach: They capture the current steps in rough bullets and ask AI to structure them into an SOP draft.
workedExample.result: The process becomes reviewable and sharable.
workedExample.verification: The operators walk through the draft step by step to catch missing exceptions.

doThisNow.task: Choose one undocumented process and gather the rough source notes for an SOP draft.
steps: List who should validate the draft.

commonMistakes: Trying to document from memory only.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to shorten prep, synthesis, and communication loops without removing judgment.
roleVariants.writer: Push for clearer source material, tighter briefs, and reusable language patterns.
roleVariants.analyst: Separate analysis from storytelling so you can verify numbers before sharing narratives.
roleVariants.operator: Build repeatable handoff-ready outputs instead of one-off conversations.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Create SOPs People Will Actually Use
lessons.subtitle: Useful SOPs are specific, scannable, and built around decisions and exceptions.
lessons.objective: Draft standard operating procedures that help people complete work, not just read about it.
lessons.useWhen: Useful SOPs are specific, scannable, and built around decisions and exceptions.
lessons.whyItMatters: Many SOPs fail because they describe a process abstractly rather than guiding someone through the real task.

coreConcepts.title: Write For The User
coreConcepts.text: Say who the SOP is for, what triggers it, and what outcome they should achieve.

coreConcepts.title: Include Exceptions
coreConcepts.text: Real work breaks at the edges. If known exceptions are missing, the SOP will fail in practice.

coreConcepts.title: Use Clear Step Shapes
coreConcepts.text: Steps, owners, required tools, and escalation rules should be easy to scan.

workedExample.scenario: A finance team needs a close checklist for a new team member.
workedExample.approach: They build the SOP around trigger, inputs, steps, validation points, and exception handling.
workedExample.result: The new team member can follow the work without constant shadowing.
workedExample.verification: An experienced operator tests the SOP by walking through it line by line.

doThisNow.task: Draft the sections of one SOP before asking AI to write the prose.
steps: Add at least one known exception.

commonMistakes: Writing the SOP as a narrative.
Failing to identify who the SOP is for.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to shorten prep, synthesis, and communication loops without removing judgment.
roleVariants.writer: Push for clearer source material, tighter briefs, and reusable language patterns.
roleVariants.analyst: Separate analysis from storytelling so you can verify numbers before sharing narratives.
roleVariants.operator: Build repeatable handoff-ready outputs instead of one-off conversations.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Turn Repeated Questions Into FAQs
lessons.subtitle: FAQ generation works best when the real questions come from support or operational history.
lessons.objective: Create internal or external FAQs from actual recurring questions and approved answers.
lessons.useWhen: FAQ generation works best when the real questions come from support or operational history.
lessons.whyItMatters: FAQ content is valuable when it reduces future interruptions and improves consistency of responses.

coreConcepts.title: Use Real Questions
coreConcepts.text: Support logs, chat threads, and onboarding notes are better sources than imagined FAQs.

coreConcepts.title: Keep Answers Operational
coreConcepts.text: Answers should tell people what to do, not just define terms.

coreConcepts.title: Mark Approval Boundaries
coreConcepts.text: If a topic changes often, include who owns updates and what must be verified before publishing.

workedExample.scenario: An operations team receives the same onboarding questions every week.
workedExample.approach: They pull real questions from chat and support notes, then ask AI to cluster and draft clear answers.
workedExample.result: The FAQ reflects actual confusion points.
workedExample.verification: The team checks each answer against the current process and removes any speculative guidance.

doThisNow.task: Collect five recurring questions from recent work and turn them into draft FAQ entries.
steps: Verify each answer with the process owner.

commonMistakes: Writing answers that are too broad.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to shorten prep, synthesis, and communication loops without removing judgment.
roleVariants.writer: Push for clearer source material, tighter briefs, and reusable language patterns.
roleVariants.analyst: Separate analysis from storytelling so you can verify numbers before sharing narratives.
roleVariants.operator: Build repeatable handoff-ready outputs instead of one-off conversations.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use AI To Improve Existing Processes
lessons.subtitle: Documentation can reveal where the work itself is weak.
lessons.objective: Use AI to identify ambiguity, duplication, or friction in an existing process.
lessons.useWhen: Documentation can reveal where the work itself is weak.
lessons.whyItMatters: Once a process is documented, AI can help critique it for handoff problems, unclear ownership, or unnecessary steps.

coreConcepts.title: Critique The Process, Not Just The Writing
coreConcepts.text: Ask where steps are vague, duplicated, or lacking a clear owner.

coreConcepts.title: Find Decision Points
coreConcepts.text: Good process reviews reveal where human judgment is required and where better rules would help.

coreConcepts.title: Separate Improvement From Approval
coreConcepts.text: AI can suggest changes, but process owners still decide what gets adopted.

workedExample.scenario: A service request workflow causes repeated back-and-forth.
workedExample.approach: The team asks AI to review the draft SOP for unclear steps and missing ownership.
workedExample.result: They identify two unnecessary handoffs and one missing approval gate.
workedExample.verification: They confirm the proposed fixes against how the work actually runs today.

artifact.label: Review Prompt
artifact.title: Process critique lens
items: Where do exceptions break the flow?

doThisNow.task: Ask AI to critique one process you already documented.
steps: Ask for unclear ownership and duplicate steps.
Review the suggestions with a process owner.

commonMistakes: Editing wording without examining the workflow.
Treating AI suggestions as approved changes.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to shorten prep, synthesis, and communication loops without removing judgment.

roleVariants.analyst: Separate analysis from storytelling so you can verify numbers before sharing narratives.
roleVariants.operator: Build repeatable handoff-ready outputs instead of one-off conversations.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Build A Documentation Toolkit
lessons.subtitle: Turn successful documentation workflows into repeatable assets.
lessons.objective: Create a small toolkit for SOPs, FAQs, and process reviews.
lessons.useWhen: Turn successful documentation workflows into repeatable assets.
lessons.whyItMatters: Documentation quality improves when the team reuses proven formats and review routines.

coreConcepts.title: Store The Source Pattern
coreConcepts.text: Capture the kinds of raw inputs that produce strong drafts.

coreConcepts.title: Save The Format
coreConcepts.text: A good SOP or FAQ structure can be reused across teams and topics.

coreConcepts.title: Keep A Review Checklist
coreConcepts.text: Documentation needs a standard final pass for accuracy, usability, and ownership.

workedExample.scenario: A team repeatedly documents new workflows during a process redesign.
workedExample.approach: They build a shared toolkit with prompt frames, formats, and review rules.
workedExample.result: New documentation takes less time and requires less reinvention.
workedExample.verification: The toolkit includes a requirement that every draft be tested by a real user of the process.

doThisNow.task: Save one documentation workflow as a reusable toolkit entry.

commonMistakes: Saving only prompts and not formats.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to shorten prep, synthesis, and communication loops without removing judgment.
roleVariants.writer: Push for clearer source material, tighter briefs, and reusable language patterns.
roleVariants.analyst: Separate analysis from storytelling so you can verify numbers before sharing narratives.
roleVariants.operator: Build repeatable handoff-ready outputs instead of one-off conversations.

outcomes: Draft SOPs and FAQs from real inputs.
Use documentation to improve process clarity.
nextModuleBridge: Next, carry those documentation habits into planning, coordination, and project work.
